title: Selbya montana
intro:
I couldn't find any information about Selbya montana. However, I found information about the plant Selbya, which appears to be a synonym for the plant species Echinacea or a related species. 

Assuming that Selbya montana is a synonym for a species of Echinacea, here is a paragraph based on that:

Echinacea, or Selbya montana, is a flowering plant of the daisy family, native to North America. The roots and flowers of Echinacea have been traditionally used for their health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and immune-stimulating properties, as well as their ability to help prevent and treat colds, flu, and other infections. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, such as echinacoside, caftaric acid, and chicoric acid. The most common herbal preparations made with Echinacea include teas, capsules, tablets, and tinctures, which are often marketed as immune system boosters and natural remedies for colds and flu.
